## Cases mentioned in passing

### US Navy submarine first-dive stress relief
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "US Navy submarine first-dive stress relief"
- **Anchor:** `§4.p2`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "So they don't use thermal stress relief, but they do stress relieve these welds — they do it with another type of energy. What type of energy would you use? Mechanical. The first deep dive. The first time you dive that submarine and go a little extra deep, deeper than you want the regular officers of the Navy to take it, you're going to do a proof load test, and you're going to take it down deep enough that you will mechanically relieve the residual stresses. We don't do it at temperature, we do it mechanically, by taking it down very low in the ocean. I can't tell you how low — that would..."

### PG&E v. American Bridge lawsuit (Fresno, California)
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "PG&E v. American Bridge lawsuit (Fresno, California)"
- **Anchor:** `§9.p2`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "So here's a hundred-million-dollar extortion that the Public Utilities Commission is giving to PG&E. PG&E would have been delighted to just forget about the lawsuit and put it in the rate base and spread the peanut butter all over all the citizens of California. But the Public Utilities Commission is there protecting citizens. So they go to trial. Six-week trial, I was the last witness. PG&E lost. They go back to Public Utilities Commission and say, well, the jury decided it was a foundational problem — our supports, foundations for the support pipe were inadequate, they shifted, and it had..."
